name: Scallion Cakes #1
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Unbleached flour, plus additional flour for kneading
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 10 inch cakes
1. Place the 2 cups of flour in a medium bowl. Gradually add the 3/4 cup cold water in a thin steady stream while stirring constantly until the mixture forms a rough dough.
2. Turn out the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead, adding more flour as needed to prevent sticking, until the dough is smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes. Allow the dough to rest under a kitchen towel for 15 minutes.
3. Divide the dough into two halves. Keep one half covered, and roll out the other half on a lightly floured surface to form a rectangle measuring approximately 10 x 6 inches, with the long side facing you.
4. Spread the rolled-out rectangle with about 2 tablespoons of lard. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of minced scallion, half of the minced garlic, and salt to taste. Roll the rectangle tightly toward you into a tight cylinder, pinching any seams or ends to seal. Repeat with the other piece of dough.
5. Shape each roll into a coil; fasten the end of the coil by pinching it. Refrigerate, wrapped in plastic wrap, for 30 minutes.
6. Remove the coils from the refrigerator. Using hands, roughly shape one coil into a ball on a generously floured surface. Roll out the ball to form a circle approximately 10 inches in diameter and about 1/4 inch thick, being careful not to roll over the edges of the dough.
7. Brush off any excess flour. Repeat with the remaining dough.
8. Heat 2 tablespoons of the peanut oil in a large heavy skillet over medium-high heat until fragrant. Add one cake into the hot oil and fry, shaking the pan occasionally, until the underside is golden brown, about 3 minutes.
9. Carefully turn the cake over and fry until the second side is golden brown, about 4 minutes. Using tongs or a slotted spoon, transfer the cake to paper towels to drain excess oil. Wipe the skillet clean, add the remaining oil if needed, and fry the second cake.
10. Cakes can be served warm or at room temperature. Optionally, garnish with 1 tablespoon of minced fresh coriander leaves for added flavor.
